contentBlocks.original:
सदञ्चित-मुदञ्चित निकुञ्चित पदं झलझलं-चलित मञ्जु कटकम् ।
पतञ्जलि दृगञ्जन-मनञ्जन-मचञ्चलपदं जनन भञ्जन करम् ।
कदम्बरुचिमम्बरवसं परममम्बुद कदम्ब कविडम्बक गलम्
चिदम्बुधि मणिं बुध हृदम्बुज रविं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadañchita-mudañchita nikuñchita padaṃ jhalajhalaṃ-chalita mañju kaṭakam ।
patañjali dṛgañjana-manañjana-machañchalapadaṃ janana bhañjana karam ।
kadambaruchimambaravasaṃ paramamambuda kadamba kaviḍambaka galam
chidambudhi maṇiṃ budha hṛdambuja raviṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The foot raised, the foot uplifted, the foot bent — the beautiful anklets ringing jhala-jhala. Who is the eye-salve (illuminator) of Patañjali's vision, who is untainted (añjana-free), whose base is unmoved and unshaken — may that Naṭarāja be worshipped in our hearts.
contentBlocks.meanings: सदा-अञ्चित-मुदा-अञ्चित-निकुञ्चित-पदम् (sadā-añcita-mudā-añcita-nikuñcita-padam) – whose feet are ever bent, joyfully raised, and contracted (sadā:always | añcita:bent | mudā:with joy | añcita:raised | nikuñcita:contracted | padam:foot); झल-झलं-चलित-मञ्जु-कटकम् (jhala-jhalaṃ-calita-mañju-kaṭakam) – with beautiful anklets jingling jhala-jhala (jhala-jhalam:jingling sound | calita:moving | mañju:beautiful | kaṭakam:anklet); पतञ्जलि-दृक्-अञ्जन-मन-अञ्जनम् (patañjali-dṛk-añjana-mana-añjanam) – collyrium for Patanjali's eyes and balm for the mind (patañjali:Patanjali | dṛk:eye | añjana:collyrium | manas:mind | añjanam:ointment); अचञ्चल-पदम् (acañcala-padam) – whose state is unwavering (acañcala:steady | padam:state); जनन-भञ्जन-करम् (janana-bhañjana-karam) – destroyer of the cycle of birth (janana:birth | bhañjana:destruction | karam:causing); कदम्ब-रुचिम् (kadamba-rucim) – with the lustre of Kadamba flowers (kadamba:Kadamba tree | rucim:lustre); अम्बर-वसम् (ambara-vasam) – clothed in sky/space (ambara:sky | vasam:clad); परमम् अम्बुद-कदम्ब-कविडम्बक-गलम् (paramam ambuda-kadamba-kaviḍambaka-galam) – whose throat rivals a mass of supreme rain clouds (paramam:supreme | ambuda:cloud | kadamba:cluster | kaviḍambaka:rivaling | galam:throat); चित्-अम्बुधि-मणिम् (cit-ambudhi-maṇim) – the jewel of the ocean of consciousness (cit:consciousness | ambudhi:ocean | maṇim:jewel); बुध-हृत्-अम्बुज-रविम् (budha-hṛt-ambuja-ravim) – the sun for the lotus-hearts of the wise (budha:wise | hṛt:heart | ambuja:lotus | ravim:sun); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-cidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer in the space of consciousness (para:supreme | cit:consciousness | ambara:space | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
हरं त्रिपुर भञ्जन-मनन्तकृतकङ्कण-मखण्डदय-मन्तरहितं
विरिञ्चिसुरसंहतिपुरन्धर विचिन्तितपदं तरुणचन्द्रमकुटम् ।
परं पद विखण्डितयमं भसित मण्डिततनुं मदनवञ्चन परं
चिरन्तनममुं प्रणवसञ्चितनिधिं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
haraṃ tripura bhañjana-manantakṛtakaṅkaṇa-makhaṇḍadaya-mantarahitaṃ
viriñchisurasaṃhatipurandhara vichintitapadaṃ taruṇachandramakuṭam ।
paraṃ pada vikhaṇḍitayamaṃ bhasita maṇḍitatanuṃ madanavañchana paraṃ
chirantanamamuṃ praṇavasañchitanidhiṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hail to Hara — destroyer of Tripura, who wears eternal armlets, whose compassion is unbroken and unlimited, who is hidden within (in the heart of all beings); whose feet are meditated upon by the assembly of Brahmā, the hosts of gods, and Indra himself — and in whom all the worlds find their dissolution.
contentBlocks.meanings: हरम् (haram) – Hara, the remover (haram:Shiva, the destroyer); त्रिपुर-भञ्जन (tripura-bhañjana) – destroyer of Tripura (tripura:three cities | bhañjana:destroyer); अनन्त-कृत-कङ्कण (ananta-kṛta-kaṅkaṇa) – having Ananta as a bracelet (ananta:the serpent Shesha | kṛta:made | kaṅkaṇa:bracelet); अखण्ड-दय (akhaṇḍa-daya) – of undivided compassion (akhaṇḍa:unbroken | daya:compassion); अन्तर-हितम् (antara-hitam) – devoted to inner welfare (antara:inner | hitam:welfare); विरिञ्चि-सुर-संहति-पुरन्धर-विचिन्तित-पदम् (viriñchi-sura-saṃhati-purandhara-vichintita-padam) – whose feet are meditated upon by Brahma, the host of gods, and Indra (viriñchi:Brahma | sura:gods | saṃhati:multitude | purandhara:Indra | vichintita:meditated upon | padam:feet); तरुण-चन्द्र-मकुटम् (taruṇa-chandra-makuṭam) – having the young moon as a crown (taruṇa:young | chandra:moon | makuṭam:crown); परं पद-विखण्डित-यमम् (paraṃ pada-vikhaṇḍita-yamam) – the supreme one who kicked and destroyed Yama (param:supreme | pada:foot | vikhaṇḍita:shattered | yamam:Yama); भसित-मण्डित-तनुम् (bhasita-maṇḍita-tanum) – whose body is adorned with sacred ash (bhasita:ash | maṇḍita:adorned | tanum:body); मदन-वञ्चन-परम् (madana-vañchana-param) – supreme in deceiving Cupid (madana:Cupid | vañchana:deceiving | param:supreme); चिरन्तनम् अमुम् (chirantanam amum) – that eternal one (chirantanam:eternal | amum:that one); प्रणव-सञ्चित-निधिम् (praṇava-sañchita-nidhim) – the treasure accumulated in Om (praṇava:Om | sañchita:accumulated | nidhim:treasure); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-chidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | chidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अवन्तमखिलं जगदभङ्ग गुणतुङ्गममतं धृतविधुं सुरसरित्-
तरङ्ग निकुरुम्ब धृति लम्पट जटं शमनदम्भसुहरं भवहरम् ।
शिवं दशदिगन्तर विजृम्भितकरं करलसन्मृगशिशुं पशुपतिं
हरं शशिधनञ्जयपतङ्गनयनं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avantamakhilaṃ jagadabhaṅga guṇatuṅgamamataṃ dhṛtavidhuṃ surasarit-
taraṅga nikurumba dhṛti lampaṭa jaṭaṃ śamanadambhasuharaṃ bhavaharam ।
śivaṃ daśadigantara vijṛmbhitakaraṃ karalasanmṛgaśiśuṃ paśupatiṃ
haraṃ śaśidhanañjayapataṅganayanaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The protector of all the world, of unbroken and lofty excellence of qualities, beyond thought, bearing the crescent moon — with matted locks that eagerly hold clusters of divine river (Gaṅgā) waves — the bestower of peace. May that Naṭarāja, the incomparable one, be victorious.
contentBlocks.meanings: अवन्तम् अखिलं जगत् (avantam akhilaṃ jagat) – protecting the entire universe (avantam:protecting | akhilam:entire | jagat:universe); अभङ्ग-गुण-तुङ्गम् (abhaṅga-guṇa-tuṅgam) – lofty with unbroken virtues (abhaṅga:unbroken | guṇa:virtues | tuṅgam:lofty); अमतं धृत-विधुम् (amataṃ dhṛta-vidhum) – immortal, bearing the moon (amatam:immortal | dhṛta:bearing | vidhum:moon); सुर-सरित्-तरङ्ग-निकुरुम्ब-धृति-लम्पट-जटम् (sura-sarit-taraṅga-nikurumba-dhṛti-lampaṭa-jaṭam) – whose matted locks eagerly hold the clusters of waves of the celestial river (sura-sarit:celestial river | taraṅga:waves | nikurumba:clusters | dhṛti:holding | lampaṭa:eager | jaṭam:matted locks); शमन-दम्भ-सुहरम् (śamana-dambha-suharam) – destroyer of the pride of Yama (śamana:Yama | dambha:pride | suharam:destroyer); भव-हरम् (bhava-haram) – remover of worldly existence (bhava:worldly existence | haram:remover); शिवम् (śivam) – the auspicious one (śivam:auspicious); दश-दिक्-अन्तर-विजृम्भित-करम् (daśa-dik-antara-vijṛmbhita-karam) – whose hands expand across the ten directions (daśa:ten | dik:directions | antara:within | vijṛmbhita:expanded | karam:hands); कर-लसत्-मृग-शिशुम् (kara-lasat-mṛga-śiśum) – with a deer fawn shining in hand (kara:hand | lasat:shining | mṛga:deer | śiśum:fawn); पशु-पतिम् (paśu-patim) – lord of creatures (paśu:creatures | patim:lord); हरम् (haram) – Hara (haram:Hara); शशि-धनञ्जय-पतङ्ग-नयनम् (śaśi-dhanañjaya-pataṅga-nayanam) – having moon, fire and sun as eyes (śaśi:moon | dhanañjaya:fire | pataṅga:sun | nayanam:eyes); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-cidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer in the space of consciousness (para:supreme | cidambara:space of consciousness | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तनवरत्नविलसत्कटककिङ्किणिझलं झलझलं झलरवं
मुकुन्दविधि हस्तगतमद्दल लयध्वनिधिमिद्धिमित नर्तन पदम् ।
शकुन्तरथ बर्हिरथ नन्दिमुख भृङ्गिरिटिसङ्घनिकटं भयहरम्
सनन्द सनक प्रमुख वन्दित पदं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantanavaratnavilasatkaṭakakiṅkiṇijhalaṃ jhalajhalaṃ jhalaravaṃ
mukundavidhi hastagatamaddala layadhvanidhimiddhimita nartana padam ।
śakuntaratha barhiratha nandimukha bhṛṅgiriṭisaṅghanikaṭaṃ bhayaharam
sananda sanaka pramukha vandita padaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Infinite new gems sparkle on his anklets, ringing jhala-jhala — jhala-jhala — with the sound of small bells. The mṛdaṅga drum is held in the hands of Mukunda (Viṣṇu) and Brahmā; its resonance — dhimid-dhimita — gives the rhythm of the cosmic dance.
contentBlocks.meanings: अनन्त-नव-रत्न-विलसत्-कटक-किङ्किणि-झलम् (ananta-nava-ratna-vilasat-kaṭaka-kiṅkiṇi-jhalam) – jingling of anklets adorned with infinite new gems (ananta:infinite | nava:new | ratna:gems | vilasat:shining | kaṭaka:bangles | kiṅkiṇi:small bells | jhalam:jingling); झल-झलं झल-रवम् (jhala-jhalaṃ jhala-ravam) – the jhala-jhala jingling sound (jhala-jhalam:jingling sound | jhala:jingling | ravam:sound); मुकुन्द-विधि-हस्त-गत-मद्दल (mukunda-vidhi-hasta-gata-maddala) – the maddala drum held in the hands of Vishnu and Brahma (mukunda:Vishnu | vidhi:Brahma | hasta:hand | gata:held | maddala:drum); लय-ध्वनि-धिम्-इद्धिम्-इत-नर्तन-पदम् (laya-dhvani-dhim-iddhim-ita-nartana-padam) – dance steps set to the rhythmic sound of dhim-iddhim (laya:rhythm | dhvani:sound | dhim:drum beat | iddhim:drum beat | ita:gone/set | nartana:dance | padam:step); शकुन्त-रथ-बर्हि-रथ (śakunta-ratha-barhi-ratha) – whose vehicles are the eagle and peacock (śakunta:eagle | ratha:vehicle | barhi:peacock | ratha:vehicle); नन्दि-मुख-भृङ्गि-रिटि-सङ्घ-निकटम् (nandi-mukha-bhṛṅgi-riṭi-saṅgha-nikaṭam) – near the assembly of Nandi, Mukha, Bhringi and Riti (nandi:Nandi | mukha:Mukha | bhṛṅgi:Bhringi | riṭi:Riti | saṅgha:assembly | nikaṭam:near); भय-हरम् (bhaya-haram) – remover of fear (bhaya:fear | haram:remover); सनन्द-सनक-प्रमुख-वन्दित-पदम् (sananda-sanaka-pramukha-vandita-padam) – whose feet are worshipped by Sananda, Sanaka and other sages (sananda:Sananda | sanaka:Sanaka | pramukha:chief/leading | vandita:worshipped | padam:feet); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-chidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | chidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तमहसं त्रिदशवन्द्य चरणं मुनि हृदन्तर वसन्तममलम्
कबन्ध वियदिन्द्ववनि गन्धवह वह्निमख बन्धुरविमञ्जु वपुषम् ।
अनन्तविभवं त्रिजगदन्तर मणिं त्रिनयनं त्रिपुर खण्डन परम्
सनन्द मुनि वन्दित पदं सकरुणं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantamahasaṃ tridaśavandya charaṇaṃ muni hṛdantara vasantamamalam
kabandha viyadindvavani gandhavaha vahnimakha bandhuravimañju vapuṣam ।
anantavibhavaṃ trijagadantara maṇiṃ trinayanaṃ tripura khaṇḍana param
sananda muni vandita padaṃ sakaruṇaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of infinite, boundless splendor — whose feet are worshipped by the thirty gods — who dwells purely in the hearts of sages — whose body consists of the five elements: sky (ākāśa), moon, earth, wind, and fire, and their oblations — salutations to that Naṭarāja of immeasurable light.
contentBlocks.meanings: अनन्त-महसम् (ananta-mahasam) – of infinite splendor (ananta:infinite | mahasam:splendor); त्रिदश-वन्द्य-चरणम् (tridaśa-vandya-charaṇam) – whose feet are worshipped by the gods (tridaśa:gods | vandya:to be worshipped | charaṇam:feet); मुनि-हृद्-अन्तर-वसन्तम् (muni-hṛd-antara-vasantam) – dwelling within the hearts of sages (muni:sage | hṛd:heart | antara:within | vasantam:dwelling); अमलम् (amalam) – pure (amalam:pure); कबन्ध-वियद्-इन्दु-अवनि-गन्धवह-वह्नि-मख-बन्धुर-विमञ्जु-वपुषम् (kabandha-viyad-indu-avani-gandhavaha-vahni-makha-bandhura-vimañju-vapuṣam) – whose beautiful form is water, space, moon, earth, wind, fire, and sacrifice (kabandha:water | viyad:space | indu:moon | avani:earth | gandhavaha:wind | vahni:fire | makha:sacrifice | bandhura:beautiful | vimañju:charming | vapuṣam:form); अनन्त-विभवम् (ananta-vibhavam) – of infinite glory (ananta:infinite | vibhavam:glory); त्रिजगद्-अन्तर-मणिम् (trijagad-antara-maṇim) – the jewel within the three worlds (trijagad:three worlds | antara:within | maṇim:jewel); त्रिनयनम् (trinayaṇam) – three-eyed (tri:three | nayanam:eyed); त्रिपुर-खण्डन-परम् (tripura-khaṇḍana-param) – supreme destroyer of Tripura (tripura:three cities | khaṇḍana:destroyer | param:supreme); सनन्द-मुनि-वन्दित-पदम् (sananda-muni-vandita-padam) – whose feet are praised by the sage Sananda (sananda:Sananda | muni:sage | vandita:praised | padam:feet); सकरुणम् (sakaruṇam) – compassionate (sakaruṇam:compassionate); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-chidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | chidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अचिन्त्यमलिवृन्द रुचि बन्धुरगलं कुरित कुन्द निकुरुम्ब धवलम्
मुकुन्द सुर वृन्द बल हन्तृ कृत वन्दन लसन्तमहिकुण्डल धरम् ।
अकम्पमनुकम्पित रतिं सुजन मङ्गलनिधिं गजहरं पशुपतिम्
धनञ्जय नुतं प्रणत रञ्जनपरं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
achintyamalivṛnda ruchi bandhuragalaṃ kurita kunda nikurumba dhavalam
mukunda sura vṛnda bala hantṛ kṛta vandana lasantamahikuṇḍala dharam ।
akampamanukampita ratiṃ sujana maṅgalanidhiṃ gajaharaṃ paśupatim
dhanañjaya nutaṃ praṇata rañjanaparaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Beyond thought — with a throat beautiful as the lustre of a swarm of bees (dark), white as clusters of jasmine blossoms — who crushes the power of the armies of gods and demons alike — that Naṭarāja, endowed with unthinkable glory, is our refuge.
contentBlocks.meanings: अचिन्त्यम् (acintyam) – inconceivable (acintyam:inconceivable); अलि-वृन्द-रुचि-बन्धुर-गलम् (ali-vṛnda-ruci-bandhura-galam) – whose throat is beautiful with the lustre of a swarm of bees (ali:bees | vṛnda:swarm | ruci:lustre | bandhura:beautiful | galam:throat); कुरित-कुन्द-निकुरुम्ब-धवलम् (kurita-kunda-nikurumba-dhavalam) – white like a cluster of blossoming jasmine flowers (kurita:blossoming | kunda:jasmine | nikurumba:cluster | dhavalam:white); मुकुन्द-सुर-वृन्द-बल-हन्तृ-कृत-वन्दन-लसन्तम् (mukunda-sura-vṛnda-bala-hantṛ-kṛta-vandana-lasantam) – shining with the salutations made by Vishnu and the hosts of gods who destroyed the strength of demons (mukunda:Vishnu | sura:gods | vṛnda:host | bala:strength | hantṛ:destroyer | kṛta:made | vandana:salutation | lasantam:shining); अहि-कुण्डल-धरम् (ahi-kuṇḍala-dharam) – wearing serpent earrings (ahi:serpent | kuṇḍala:earrings | dharam:wearing); अकम्पम् (akampam) – unshakeable (akampam:unshakeable); अनुकम्पित-रतिम् (anukampita-ratim) – delighting in compassion (anukampita:compassion | ratim:delighting); सुजन-मङ्गल-निधिम् (sujana-maṅgala-nidhim) – treasury of auspiciousness for good people (sujana:good people | maṅgala:auspiciousness | nidhim:treasury); गज-हरम् (gaja-haram) – slayer of the elephant demon (gaja:elephant | haram:slayer); पशुपतिम् (paśupatim) – Lord of beings (paśupatim:Lord of beings); धनञ्जय-नुतम् (dhanañjaya-nutam) – praised by Arjuna (dhanañjaya:Arjuna | nutam:praised); प्रणत-रञ्जन-परम् (praṇata-rañjana-param) – devoted to delighting those who bow (praṇata:those who bow | rañjana:delighting | param:devoted to); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-cidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | cidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
परं सुरवरं पुरहरं पशुपतिं जनित दन्तिमुख षण्मुखममुं
मृडं कनक पिङ्गल जटं सनक पङ्कज रविं सुमनसं हिमरुचिम् ।
असङ्घमनसं जलधि जन्मगरलं कवलयन्त मतुलं गुणनिधिम्
सनन्द वरदं शमितमिन्दु वदनं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paraṃ suravaraṃ puraharaṃ paśupatiṃ janita dantimukha ṣaṇmukhamamuṃ
mṛḍaṃ kanaka piṅgala jaṭaṃ sanaka paṅkaja raviṃ sumanasaṃ himaruchim ।
asaṅghamanasaṃ jaladhi janmagaralaṃ kavalayanta matulaṃ guṇanidhim
sananda varadaṃ śamitamindu vadanaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The supreme — the best of gods, destroyer of cities (Tripura), lord of animals (Paśupati), father of the elephant-faced (Gaṇeśa) and the six-faced (Kārttikeya), the gentle one (Mṛḍa), with golden-tawny matted locks — who is the sun illuminating the lotus of sage Sanaka and the knowers of Brahman.
contentBlocks.meanings: परम् सुर-वरम् (param sura-varam) – the supreme one, best among gods (param:supreme | sura:god | varam:best); पुर-हरम् (pura-haram) – destroyer of the cities (pura:cities | haram:destroyer); पशुपतिम् (paśupatim) – lord of beings (paśupati:lord of creatures); जनित-दन्ति-मुख-षण्-मुखम् अमुम् (janita-danti-mukha-ṣaṇ-mukham amum) – that one who fathered elephant-faced and six-faced ones (janita:who begot | danti-mukha:elephant-faced Ganesha | ṣaṇ-mukham:six-faced Kartikeya | amum:that one); मृडम् (mṛḍam) – the gracious one (mṛḍam:gracious, Shiva); कनक-पिङ्गल-जटम् (kanaka-piṅgala-jaṭam) – having golden-tawny matted hair (kanaka:golden | piṅgala:tawny | jaṭam:matted hair); सनक-पङ्कज-रविम् (sanaka-paṅkaja-ravim) – the sun to the lotus of Sanaka (sanaka:sage Sanaka | paṅkaja:lotus | ravim:sun); सुमनसम् (sumanasam) – of good mind, benevolent (sumanasam:good-minded); हिम-रुचिम् (hima-ruchim) – having moon-like radiance (hima:cool, moon | ruchim:lustre); असङ्घ-मनसम् (asaṅgha-manasam) – with unattached mind (asaṅgha:unattached | manasam:mind); जलधि-जन्म-गरलम् कवलयन्तम् (jaladhi-janma-garalam kavalayantam) – swallowing the poison born of the ocean (jaladhi:ocean | janma:born | garalam:poison | kavalayantam:swallowing); अतुलम् (atulam) – incomparable (atulam:matchless); गुण-निधिम् (guṇa-nidhim) – treasure of virtues (guṇa:virtues | nidhim:treasure); सानन्द-वरदम् (sānanda-varadam) – blissfully granting boons (sānanda:with bliss | varadam:boon-giver); शमित-मिन्दु-वदनम् (śamita-m-indu-vadanam) – with face serene like the moon (śamita:calmed | indu:moon | vadanam:face); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-cidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | cidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अजं क्षितिरथं भुजगपुङ्गवगुणं कनक शृङ्गि धनुषं करलसत्
कुरङ्ग पृथु टङ्क परशुं रुचिर कुङ्कुम रुचिं डमरुकं च दधतम् ।
मुकुन्द विशिखं नमदवन्ध्य फलदं निगम वृन्द तुरगं निरुपमं
स चण्डिकममुं झटिति संहृतपुरं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajaṃ kṣitirathaṃ bhujagapuṅgavaguṇaṃ kanaka śṛṅgi dhanuṣaṃ karalasat
kuraṅga pṛthu ṭaṅka paraśuṃ ruchira kuṅkuma ruchiṃ ḍamarukaṃ cha dadhatam ।
mukunda viśikhaṃ namadavandhya phaladaṃ nigama vṛnda turagaṃ nirupamaṃ
sa chaṇḍikamamuṃ jhaṭiti saṃhṛtapuraṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The unborn one, whose chariot is the earth (kṣiti-ratha), whose bowstring is the king of serpents (bhujaga-puṅgava-guṇa), whose bow is the golden-peaked mountain — who holds in his shining hands the deer, the large chisel, the axe — and who is adorned with lovely vermilion and divine ornaments.
contentBlocks.meanings: अजम् (ajam) – the goat (ajam:goat); क्षिति-रथम् (kṣiti-ratham) – having the earth as chariot (kṣiti:earth | ratham:chariot); भुजग-पुङ्गव-गुणम् (bhujaga-puṅgava-guṇam) – having the serpent king as bowstring (bhujaga:serpent | puṅgava:chief | guṇam:string); कनक-शृङ्गि-धनुषम् (kanaka-śṛṅgi-dhanuṣam) – having the golden-peaked Mount Meru as bow (kanaka:golden | śṛṅgi:peaked | dhanuṣam:bow); कर-लसत् (kara-lasat) – shining in the hand (kara:hand | lasat:shining); कुरङ्ग (kuraṅga) – deer (kuraṅga:deer); पृथु-टङ्क-परशुम् (pṛthu-ṭaṅka-paraśum) – large chisel and axe (pṛthu:large | ṭaṅka:chisel | paraśum:axe); रुचिर-कुङ्कुम-रुचिम् (ruchira-kuṅkuma-ruchim) – having beautiful saffron complexion (ruchira:beautiful | kuṅkuma:saffron | ruchim:lustre); डमरुकं च दधतम् (ḍamarukaṃ cha dadhatam) – and holding the drum (ḍamarukam:small drum | cha:and | dadhatam:holding); मुकुन्द-विशिखम् (mukunda-viśikham) – having Vishnu as arrow (mukunda:Vishnu | viśikham:arrow); नमत्-अवन्ध्य-फलदम् (namat-avandhya-phaladam) – bestowing unfailing fruit to those who bow (namat:bowing | avandhya:unfailing | phaladam:fruit-giver); निगम-वृन्द-तुरगम् (nigama-vṛnda-turagam) – having the collection of Vedas as horses (nigama:Vedas | vṛnda:collection | turagam:horses); निरुपमम् (nirupamam) – incomparable (nirupamam:without comparison); स-चण्डिकम् (sa-chaṇḍikam) – along with Chandika (sa:with | chaṇḍikam:Chandika); अमुम् (amum) – that one (amum:that); झटिति संहृत-पुरम् (jhaṭiti saṃhṛta-puram) – who instantly destroyed the Tripura (jhaṭiti:instantly | saṃhṛta:destroyed | puram:cities); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-chidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer of Chidambaram (para:supreme | chidambara:Chidambaram | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
अनङ्गपरिपन्थिनमजं क्षिति धुरन्धरमलं करुणयन्तमखिलं
ज्वलन्तमनलं दधतमन्तकरिपुं सततमिन्द्र सुरवन्दितपदम् ।
उदञ्चदरविन्दकुल बन्धुशत बिम्बरुचि संहति सुगन्धि वपुषं
पतञ्जलि नुतं प्रणव पञ्जर शुकं पर चिदम्बर नटं हृदि भज ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anaṅgaparipanthinamajaṃ kṣiti dhurandharamalaṃ karuṇayantamakhilaṃ
jvalantamanalaṃ dadhatamantakaripuṃ satatamindra suravanditapadam ।
udañchadaravindakula bandhuśata bimbaruchi saṃhati sugandhi vapuṣaṃ
patañjali nutaṃ praṇava pañjara śukaṃ para chidambara naṭaṃ hṛdi bhaja ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The enemy of Kāma (Kāma-destroyer), the unborn, the bearer of the world, the fully compassionate one — who holds blazing fire, the enemy of Yama (Mṛtyuñjaya, conqueror of death), always honored by Indra — who makes all darkness flee — may that Naṭarāja be our eternal refuge.
contentBlocks.meanings: अनङ्ग-परिपन्थिनम् (anaṅga-paripanthinam) – enemy of Ananga/Kamadeva (anaṅga:bodiless one/Cupid | paripanthinam:enemy); अजम् (ajam) – unborn (ajam:unborn); क्षिति-धुरन्धरम् (kṣiti-dhurandharam) – supporter of the earth (kṣiti:earth | dhurandharam:one who bears the burden); अलम् करुणयन्तम् अखिलम् (alam karuṇayantam akhilam) – abundantly bestowing compassion on all (alam:abundantly | karuṇayantam:showing compassion | akhilam:all); ज्वलन्तम् अनलम् दधतम् (jvalantam analam dadhatam) – holding blazing fire (jvalantam:blazing | analam:fire | dadhatam:holding); अन्तक-रिपुम् (antaka-ripum) – enemy of Yama/Death (antaka:Death | ripum:enemy); सततम् इन्द्र-सुर-वन्दित-पदम् (satatam indra-sura-vandita-padam) – whose feet are always worshipped by Indra and the gods (satatam:always | indra:Indra | sura:gods | vandita:worshipped | padam:feet); उदञ्छत्-अरविन्द-कुल-बन्धु-शत-बिम्ब-रुचि-संहति-सुगन्धि-वपुषम् (udañchat-aravinda-kula-bandhu-śata-bimba-ruchi-saṃhati-sugandhi-vapuṣam) – whose fragrant body has the radiance of a hundred rising suns, friends of the lotus family (udañchat:rising | aravinda:lotus | kula:family | bandhu:friend/sun | śata:hundred | bimba:disc | ruchi:radiance | saṃhati:collection | sugandhi:fragrant | vapuṣam:body); पतञ्जलि-नुतम् (patañjali-nutam) – praised by Patanjali (patañjali:Patanjali | nutam:praised); प्रणव-पञ्जर-शुकम् (praṇava-pañjara-śukam) – the parrot in the cage of Om (praṇava:Om | pañjara:cage | śukam:parrot); पर-चिदम्बर-नटम् (para-chidambara-naṭam) – the supreme dancer in the space of consciousness (para:supreme | chidambara:space of consciousness | naṭam:dancer); हृदि भज (hṛdi bhaja) – worship in the heart (hṛdi:in the heart | bhaja:worship);

contentBlocks.original:
इति स्तवममुं भुजगपुङ्गव कृतं प्रतिदिनं पठति यः कृतमुखः
सदः प्रभुपद द्वितयदर्शनपदं सुललितं चरण शृङ्ग रहितम् ।
सरः प्रभव सम्भव हरित्पति हरिप्रमुख दिव्यनुत शङ्करपदं
स गच्छति परं न तु जनुर्जलनिधिं परमदुःखजनकं दुरितदम् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti stavamamuṃ bhujagapuṅgava kṛtaṃ pratidinaṃ paṭhati yaḥ kṛtamukhaḥ
sadaḥ prabhupada dvitayadarśanapadaṃ sulalitaṃ charaṇa śṛṅga rahitam ।
saraḥ prabhava sambhava haritpati haripramukha divyanuta śaṅkarapadaṃ
sa gachChati paraṃ na tu janurjalanidhiṃ paramaduḥkhajanakaṃ duritadam ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites daily this praise composed by the king of serpents (Patañjali) — with a face full of devotion — will attain the sight of the two lotus-feet of the lord of the divine assembly (Naṭarāja), adorned with beautiful, graceful garlands. Such a one will be liberated.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति स्तवम् अमुम् (iti stavam amum) – this hymn thus (iti:thus | stavam:hymn | amum:this); भुजग-पुङ्गव-कृतम् (bhujaga-puṅgava-kṛtam) – composed by the foremost of serpents (bhujaga:serpent | puṅgava:foremost | kṛtam:composed); प्रतिदिनम् पठति यः (pratidinam paṭhati yaḥ) – whoever recites daily (pratidinam:daily | paṭhati:recites | yaḥ:whoever); कृत-मुखः (kṛta-mukhaḥ) – with devoted mouth (kṛta:made ready | mukhaḥ:mouth); सदः प्रभु-पद-द्वितय-दर्शन-पदम् (sadaḥ prabhu-pada-dvitaya-darśana-padam) – the state of seeing the two feet of the Lord of the assembly (sadaḥ:of assembly | prabhu:Lord | pada:feet | dvitaya:pair | darśana:seeing | padam:state); सुललितम् (sulalitam) – most graceful (sulalitam:most graceful); चरण-शृङ्ग-रहितम् (caraṇa-śṛṅga-rahitam) – devoid of feet and horns (caraṇa:feet | śṛṅga:horns | rahitam:devoid); सरः-प्रभव-सम्भव (saraḥ-prabhava-sambhava) – born from the lake-born one (saraḥ:lake | prabhava:origin | sambhava:born); हरित्-पति (harit-pati) – Lord of the green ones (harit:green | pati:lord); हरि-प्रमुख (hari-pramukha) – Hari and others foremost (hari:Hari | pramukha:foremost); दिव्य-नुत (divya-nuta) – praised by divine beings (divya:divine | nuta:praised); शङ्कर-पदम् (śaṅkara-padam) – the state of Shankara (śaṅkara:Shankara | padam:state); स गच्छति परम् (sa gacchati param) – he attains the Supreme (sa:he | gacchati:goes | param:Supreme); न तु जनुः-जल-निधिम् (na tu januḥ-jala-nidhim) – not the ocean of birth (na:not | tu:but | januḥ:birth | jala:water | nidhim:ocean); परम-दुःख-जनकम् (parama-duḥkha-janakam) – producer of extreme sorrow (parama:extreme | duḥkha:sorrow | janakam:producer); दुरितदम् (duritadam) – giver of sin (durita:sin | dam:giver);
